---
title: IRS TIN / Tax-Compliance Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: irs-tin-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# IRS TIN / Tax-Compliance Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The IRS TIN / Tax-Compliance Attribute Service Adapter is the **IRS attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes attribute-disclosure events from the IRS — the federal authority of record for TIN / EIN per **26 U.S.C. §6109** — through TIN Matching (Pub 2108A), Income Verification Express Service (IVES), and federal tax-compliance check APIs.

The adapter operates under one of the strictest disclosure regimes in the federal attribute-service family. **26 U.S.C. §6103** governs return-information confidentiality, and every entitlement must cite the §6103 authorization in addition to the Privacy Act SORN. A consuming engagement that lacks the §6103 authorization is rejected at the entitlement-validation layer before any IRS API call is issued.

Three primary functions:

1. **TIN Matching observation.** Observes TIN / EIN match disclosures via IRS TIN Matching (Pub 2108A) for payment-recipient verification, emitting canonical claims per match attempt.
2. **IVES income verification ingestion.** Captures income-record disclosures from the Income Verification Express Service. IVES is IRS-hosted but the underlying records are SSA-derived; the adapter pairs with [`ssa-attribute-service`](../ssa-attribute-service/ssa-attribute-service.html) so provenance cites both authorities.
3. **Federal tax-compliance check ingestion.** Wraps the documented federal tax-compliance check APIs for engagements that require the attribute (federal contracting, grants, federal-financial-assistance eligibility).

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `tin-matching`, `ein-verification`, `federal-tax-compliance`, `ives-income-verification`

**Reads:** IRS TIN Matching (Pub 2108A), Income Verification Express Service (IVES), federal tax-compliance check APIs. Access requires the consuming entitlement plus the §6103 authorization in addition to the Privacy Act SORN; both citations recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`.

**Emits:** `irs-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim with disclosed attribute, IRS channel, statute citation, authority of record), `irs-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with entitlement reference, §6103 authorization, Privacy Act SORN, IRS disclosure id), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** issue or modify TIN / EIN records (IRS is the authority of record), bulk-pull tax-return data (per-subject disclosures only), access taxpayer financial detail beyond what the disclosure surfaces, or store return-information outside the §6103-bounded retention and encryption envelope.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | TIN / EIN-verification claims supplement identification when a tax-anchored attribute is required by a downstream business process. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | IRS disclosure id is the stable identifier for each disclosure event. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope, entitlement reference, §6103 authorization, and SORN citation. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All IRS channels are TLS-protected. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Validation uses FIPS-validated cryptographic libraries; return-information at rest is encrypted per §6103 controls. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

IRS is the federal authority of record for TIN / EIN per 26 U.S.C. §6109. This adapter observes attribute-disclosure events through IRS TIN Matching (Pub 2108A), Income Verification Express Service (IVES), and federal tax compliance check APIs. Disclosures are tightly governed by 26 U.S.C. §6103 (return information confidentiality) — every entitlement must cite the §6103 authorization in addition to the Privacy Act SORN. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family — pairs with [`ssa-attribute-service`](../ssa-attribute-service/ssa-attribute-service.html) (IVES is IRS-hosted but SSA-derived) and [`gsa-sam-attribute-service`](../gsa-sam-attribute-service/gsa-sam-attribute-service.html) (federal-contracting eligibility).

ZTMM pillars: identity, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
